eslint-js/no-restricted-syntax.message: Do not write function return types. Let TypeScript infer them; when inference is unclear, name intermediate values or extract a schema/type alias.
Do not annotate const/var values. Let the initializer infer the type; use `satisfies` to check an object shape without changing inference.
Only annotate `let` as `unknown` when the value truly starts unknown. Otherwise initialize from a typed value, narrow from `unknown`, or use `satisfies`.
Do not annotate class fields. Initialize from typed values or use `satisfies` on object literals.
Do not use type casts, except `as const`. Prefer `satisfies` for shape checks, schema decoding for unknown input, or a real narrowing function.
Do not use angle-bracket type casts. Prefer `satisfies`, schema decoding, or a narrowing function.
Do not use non-null assertions. Check the value and return or fail early so TypeScript can narrow it.
